user_query:
I need to quickly verify that my office PC is ready to serve our Mandarin-speaking tour groups and then brief IT on any gaps. Please build a small, self-contained validator that reads the policy from input/policy.yaml and:

- Runs local system checks without changing settings:
  - Time zone check: confirm the system is set to Europe/London. Prefer parsing "timedatectl"; if unavailable, fall back to "date +%Z" and use UK abbreviations (GMT/BST) to decide. Handle missing commands gracefully.
  - Chinese locale check: confirm at least one Chinese locale is installed by inspecting "locale -a" for any of the entries in the policy.
  - Chinese font check: attempt to detect presence of Chinese-capable fonts using fontconfig (e.g., "fc-list :lang=zh" or by matching a policy hint). If the command is missing, capture the error and mark the status accordingly.

- For each check, analyze command output and any error messages to classify status as "pass", "fail", or "unknown" and capture a concise evidence snippet (e.g., the matching line or the exact error like "command not found"). Do not hardcode outcomes; decide based on what the machine reports.

- Produce these artifacts under the workspace:
  1) output/validation_report.json — JSON with:
     - policy: echo of the loaded policy values so we can cross-check.
     - checks: an object with keys timezone, chinese_locale, chinese_font. Each must have:
       - status: one of "pass" | "fail" | "unknown"
       - evidence: short string with the key line(s) or error observed
       - commands_run: array of the exact commands attempted for that check
     - summary: include pass_count, fail_count, unknown_count, and overall_status ("all_passed", "issues_detected", or "inconclusive").
     - generated_at: ISO 8601 timestamp.
  2) output/validation_raw.log — a plain-text log capturing full stdout/stderr for all commands run in order, with simple section headers per check.
  3) output/email_draft.txt — a plain-text email draft addressed using recipients from input/contacts.json that summarizes results for IT. Include:
     - To: and CC: using the provided contacts
     - Subject: "PC readiness check for Chinese guests — [all passed|issues detected|inconclusive]"
     - A brief purpose line
     - A summary line with pass/fail/unknown counts
     - A bullet list per check with: status, expected vs. observed, and an evidence snippet
     - Next steps: if any fail/unknown, request IT to advise fixes; otherwise, confirm readiness
     - A note stating that full command output is saved at output/validation_raw.log and the JSON report at output/validation_report.json

Implementation notes:
- You may use Bash or Python. The validator must read input/policy.yaml and input/contacts.json. It must run the validation commands locally and handle missing commands robustly by marking the relevant check as "unknown" and including the error in evidence.
- Run your validator locally and produce the three required outputs exactly at the paths above. Results will vary by machine; correctness will be judged by structure, use of the policy, and faithful capture/analysis of command outputs and errors.

Inputs are in input/policy.yaml and input/contacts.json; please do not add external dependencies or require network access. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
